articleTitle: Prospective biomarkers of posttraumatic stress disorder in children and adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
takeaways: Elevated heart rate following trauma exposure showed the most consistent association with increased risk of PTSD persistence in children and adolescents across multiple studies
Only 21 studies out of 2039 initially identified articles met quality criteria for analyzing biomarkers that predict PTSD development in youth, indicating a significant gap in reliable research

The systematic review found limited evidence for using hormonal, immunological, or cardiovascular biomarkers to predict which trauma-exposed children will develop PTSD, with most studies focusing on natural recovery patterns

Significant methodological inconsistencies across studies in trauma type classification, participant selection, and biomarker measurement techniques prevented reliable conclusions about most potential PTSD predictors
Research from low-and-middle income countries was notably absent despite these regions having high rates of chronic trauma exposure in children and adolescents
